content: Most existing explorations frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative improvement pipeline, but integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research can unlock a new multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that maintains the original method's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while further boosting output quality. This framework would prompt the base LLM to generate critical feedback from distinct inconsistent reasoning stances (e.g., skeptical domain expert, end-user, efficiency optimizer) in each refinement iteration, rather than relying on a single generic self-feedback pass.
